documents.text: Southern Hills Hospital and Medical Center (COCSNV) Hospitalist Discharge Summary REPORT#0401-0522 REPORT STATUS: Signed DATE:04/01/26 TIME: 1149 PATIENT: TREATMAN, MARY UNIT #: H000631706 ACCOUNT#: H89684160771 ROOM/BED: H.528-A DOB: 11/15/47 AGE: 78 SEX: F ATTEND: Zdrnja, Milan MD ADM DT: 03/30/26 AUTHOR: GHASHAM, HUSSEIN DO R1 REP SRV DT: 04/01/26 REP SRV TM: 1149 * ALL edits or amendments must be made on the electronic/computer document * General Information Discharge date: 03/31/26 Discharge diagnosis: Left hip displaced intertrochanteric fracture Bilateral hip osteoarthritis History of enlarged ventricles History of balance issues with multiple falls History of vertigo History of incontinence Hospital course: Patient is a 78-year-old female patient with a past medical history of vertigo, chronic balance issues due to enlarged ventricles that she uses a cane/walker/wheelchair to get around most days, multiple falls and incontinence who presents to the emergency department today after a fall that happened shortly prior to arrival. She presents emergency department today via EMS. Patient reports she has had more than 12 falls in the last several months. Patient reports that today she was walking around using her walker outside in her garden and fell landing on her left hip. She reports she did not hit her head. Patient denied dizziness, palpitations or chest pain prior to fall. No LOC or sudden weakness. she reports she had a glass of white wine when she was walking outside. Does not report any chest pain abdominal pain constipation or diarrhea. Patient reports she had her similar chronic balance issues that cause her to fall. She has been dealing with the balance issues for last several years. Assessment: Left hip displaced intertrochanteric fracture Bilateral hip osteoarthritis History of enlarged ventricles History of balance issues with multiple falls History of vertigo History of incontinence Plan Continuous cardiopulmonary monitoring Orthopedic surgery consulted, appreciate recs NPO at midnight, pending left hip surgery tomorrow Page 1 of 6 Patient: TREATMAN, MARY Unit#: H000631706 Date: 04/01/26 Acct#: H89684160771 Nonweightbearing left lower extremity Hold anticoagulants Fall precautions Post surgery rehab versus sniff Maintenance IV fluids Type and Screen Pain control Oxygen as needed to maintain saturations above 92% Electrolyte replacement per protocol Supportive care as needed Reconcile home medications and continue: Patient does not take any home meds PT/OT Code status: DNR/DNI Diet: NPO except meds DVT: SCD's GI PPX: None Dispo: Pending surgical evaluation/intervention Discussed with attending physician 03/30/2026 Patient seen and evaluated at bedside. NPO. Plan for left hip cephalomedullary nail scheduled today at 2:00 p.m.. Pain control PRNs on. Patient most likely will postoperatively need placement in rehab versus SNF. 04/01/2026 Pt seen and evaluated at bedside. Pt Left hip cephulmedullary nail surgery completed. Pt pain is controlled. Pt recieved dose of vancomycin post-op. Plan for aspirin 81mg BID for 6 weeks, Follouw up in 2 weeks. On day of discharge, patient had stable labs and vitals. Pt was cleared by orthopadic surgery. Pt sent out on home meds and aspirin 81mg BID for 6 weeks. Pt disposition to home w/ home PT, patient in agreement. Pt to f/u with Ortho Surg in 2 weeks, and PCP. Pt Pt was encouraged to see Neurology inpatient due to her hx of enlarged ventricles and gait dysfunction, but patient refused, but said she would consider following up in the outpt setting. Pt sent with referral and instructed to follow up with Desert Neurology. Answered any questinos or concerns appropriately. Consultants: Orthopedic surgery Med Rec Med Rec Page 2 of 6 Patient: TREATMAN, MARY Unit#: H000631706 Date: 04/01/26 Acct#: H89684160771 Discharge meds: Start taking the following new medications: ASPIRIN (ASPIRIN EC) 81 MG TAB.DR 81 MILLIGRAM ORAL TWICE DAILY Days = 30 Qty = 60 No Refills Objective VS/I&O Last Documented: Result Date Time Pulse Ox 93 04/01 1101 B/P 101/63 04/01 1101 B/P Mean 76.0 04/01 1101 O2 Delivery Room air 04/01 1101 Temp 97.5 04/01 1101 Pulse 86 04/01 1101 Resp 16 04/01 1101 FiO2 21 03/31 2011 O2 Flow Rate 0 03/31 2011 24 hour I&O ending at 0700: 04/01 0700 03/31 1900 Intake Total 120 320 Output Total 475 Balance 120 -155 Intake, Oral 120 320 Number Voids 2 1 Output, Urine 475 General appearance: alert, awake, oriented Head/Eyes: atraumatic, normocephalic ENT: moist mucosal membranes, normal ear left Neck: full range of motion, non-tender Cardiovascular: normal heart sounds, regular rate & rhythm Respiratory: aerating well, clear to auscultation Abdomen: soft, no distention Genitourinary: no flank pain Page 3 of 6 Patient: TREATMAN, MARY Unit#: H000631706 Date: 04/01/26 Acct#: H89684160771 Rectal: deferred Extremities: moves all, normal capillary refill Musculoskeletal: Left lower extremity is shortened and slightly externally rotated.
